authoritative_sources.title: Food Code 2022
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA Food Code is the authoritative U.S. model retail food safety code used by many jurisdictions. It directly constrains grocery operations when receiving, storage, cold holding, hot holding, contamination, employee health, sanitation, or saleability conditions affect whether work can proceed.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: food-safety hold, cold-chain, contamination, sanitation, employee-health, and saleability boundary handling
used_for: stop conditions for unsafe or uncertain food handling
cold-chain and department operations evidence
handoff to food-safety authority instead of operational release

authoritative_sources.title: Voluntary National Retail Food Regulatory Program Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's Retail Program Standards are official public guidance for retail food program inspections, risk-factor control, corrective action, complaint readiness, and documentation. They are authoritative for defining evidence expectations and escalation discipline in grocery retail operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: retail food program documentation, corrective-action, complaint, and inspection-readiness handling
used_for: documentation expectations for safety-sensitive grocery operations
corrective-action and complaint routing discipline
evidence-backed escalation rather than silent closure

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Handbook 130 - Uniform Laws and Regulations in the Areas of Legal Metrology and Fuel Quality
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST Office of Weights and Measures publishes the public baseline used by jurisdictions for retail price verification, unit pricing, method of sale, labeling, and ecommerce pricing controls. It is directly authoritative for grocery operational blockers involving shelf/POS mismatches, weighed items, unit price, PLU, and ecommerce price records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: price verification, method-of-sale, unit-price, weighed-item, and ecommerce price evidence review
used_for: pricing and unit-price boundaries during order execution
scale, PLU, random-weight, and method-of-sale prerequisite checks
routing price conflicts to authorized pricing owners

authoritative_sources.title: Mail, Internet, or Telephone Order Merchandise Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC guidance is authoritative for online and phone order commitments, delay notices, cancellation rights, and prompt refunds. It constrains grocery ecommerce fulfillment when delivery windows, substitutions, cancellations, payment mismatches, or refund-intake records affect the customer.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: online grocery order, delivery, delay, cancellation, substitution, and refund-intake exception handling
used_for: order and delivery commitment boundaries
customer-impact routing for delayed, canceled, or mismatched ecommerce orders
refund-intake escalation instead of in-lane approval

authoritative_sources.title: SNAP Retailer Training Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USDA FNS administers SNAP retailer authorization and publishes official retailer training materials. It is authoritative for operational handling of eligible food, EBT/SNAP transaction controls, coupon handling, retailer equipment, and compliance prerequisites in grocery operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: EBT/SNAP tender, eligible-item, coupon, refund-intake, and point-of-sale constraint handling
used_for: SNAP/EBT tender constraints
eligible-item and checkout boundary handling
routing program exceptions outside routine operations

authoritative_sources.title: SNAP Fraud Prevention
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USDA FNS is authoritative for SNAP program integrity and retailer fraud response. Its guidance supports escalation when suspected trafficking, ineligible sales, EBT abuse, or scam indicators appear in grocery customer, tender, or refund workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SNAP/EBT fraud, program-integrity, tender-abuse, and loss-prevention escalation
used_for: fraud-sensitive tender routing
program-integrity escalation boundaries
refusal to treat suspicious EBT/SNAP cases as routine service recovery

authoritative_sources.title: Recalls, Market Withdrawals, & Safety Alerts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA publishes official recall, market withdrawal, and safety alert records for FDA-regulated products sold by grocery retailers. It is authoritative for stop-sale, recall pull, substitution, customer-contact, and completion blockers for affected products.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: FDA-regulated recall, withdrawal, stop-sale, substitution, and customer-impact handling
used_for: recall and stop-sale blockers
customer-impact escalation for affected orders
refusal to close or fulfill affected items without recall evidence

authoritative_sources.title: Recalls and Public Health Alerts
authoritative_sources.publisher: USDA Food Safety and Inspection Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USDA FSIS is the federal public health authority for meat, poultry, and certain egg product recalls. Its records are authoritative for grocery meat, deli, poultry, and egg-product operational stop conditions, customer-impact handling, and completion blockers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: meat, poultry, and egg product recall or public health alert handling
used_for: FSIS-regulated department recall evidence
stop-sale and order substitution blockers
customer-impact and food-safety routing

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 General Specifications Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized global standards body for retail identifiers and barcodes. Its specifications are authoritative for UPC, GTIN, barcode, coupon, refund receipt, lot/date data, and record-linkage vocabulary across grocery POS, OMS, item master, receiving, inventory, and recall records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SKU, UPC, GTIN, barcode, item-master, coupon, refund receipt, lot, and record-linkage review
used_for: item identity vocabulary and record linkage
barcode, coupon, receipt, and inventory artifact handling
preventing product-identity assumptions from partial records

authoritative_sources.title: PCI DSS
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized standards body for payment-card security. PCI DSS is authoritative for protecting payment data when grocery operations use receipts, tender records, card tokens, service tickets, chargeback artifacts, or fraud-sensitive payment evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment-record, tender, chargeback, card-data, and fraud-sensitive evidence handling
used_for: payment-data minimization and routing
fraud-sensitive service ticket boundaries
refusal to expose or adjudicate protected payment details

workflow_stages.name: Execution intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm store, banner, department, channel, jurisdiction, customer type, order or service-ticket record, product identifiers, tender type, and tenant authority context before accepting work.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior

workflow_stages.name: Record and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate POS, OMS, CRM, service desk, item master, inventory, receiving, staging, delivery, recall, food-safety, pricing, promotion, loyalty, coupon, and tender records before operational progression.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior

workflow_stages.name: Receiving, inventory, and replenishment execution
workflow_stages.description: Review receiving quantities, vendor invoices, lot/date codes, item-master mapping, on-hand inventory, shrink, spoilage, replenishment tasks, department production records, and allocation state to determine advance, hold, or rework.
source_classes: Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Order, pickup, delivery, substitution, and payment reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile online grocery orders, pick lists, substitution preferences, staged totes, cold-chain proof, pickup or delivery handoff, POS authorization, payment capture, CRM notes, and service tickets.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Safety, recall, tender, fraud, and policy boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve stop-sale, recall, food-safety, EBT/SNAP, payment-data, fraud, loss-prevention, refund, promotion, franchise, and policy boundaries when they determine whether work can proceed.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue, completion evidence, and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Move blocked work into explicit exception states, route to the correct owner, and close only when tenant-specific controls and source-of-truth completion records support closure.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Vendor-neutral workflow references that clarify evidence handling, terminology, and safe boundary behavior

artifacts: POS receipts, transaction logs, tender records, front-end exception logs, cash-office notes, CRM records, service tickets, refund-intake records, and customer communication notes

OMS orders, pickup and delivery windows, shopper pick lists, substitution preferences, staged tote records, cold-chain staging proof, pickup handoff records, delivery handoff records, and cancellation records

item-master records, SKU, UPC, GTIN, PLU, lot code, date code, random-weight or catch-weight data, package size, scale labels, shelf tags, price files, promotion records, loyalty offers, and digital coupon rules

inventory states, on-hand and on-order counts, allocation records, replenishment tasks, receiving logs, vendor invoices, department production records, shrink and spoilage notes, and out-of-stock records

recall notices, market withdrawal records, public health alerts, stop-sale directives, food-safety hold notes, temperature-control blockers, sanitation or contamination notes, and recall-pull completion records

SNAP/EBT tender notes, payment-data references, chargeback records, fraud or loss-prevention holds, repeated-refund indicators, coupon-abuse notes, service-recovery records, and franchise policy notes
systems_of_record: item master or product information management system
payment processor or tender exception system

allowed: Classify grocery operations requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, system retrieval, and source context required to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, escalate, return, or complete grocery operations work based on documented lifecycle state and evidence.
Prepare execution status updates, completion records, exception queue notes, retail workflow summaries, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
escalate: Required order, payment, delivery, pickup, service, item, inventory, receiving, pricing, promotion, refund, recall, food-safety, fraud, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.

The request requires price override, promotion override, refund approval, service credit approval, SNAP/EBT exception, food-safety disposition, recalled-product release, payment dispute adjudication, fraud adjudication, legal interpretation, or executive approval.
A public source, tenant SOP, franchise rule, jurisdictional rule, service policy, tender control, or program rule materially conflicts with another controlling source.
Customer harm, food safety, active recall, fraud, loss prevention, payment security, refund leakage, or policy exception determines the answer.
refuse: Mark work complete without evidence of completion.
Override pricing, refund, promotion, tender, safety, fraud, payment, or policy controls without documented authority.
Release product to sale, pickup, delivery, substitution, refund resolution, or service closure when order, payment, safety, recall, fraud, or completion evidence remains unresolved.

domain_failure_modes: pickup or delivery work is marked complete even though staged tote, handoff, POS payment, delivery, or service-desk closure evidence is missing
a SKU, UPC, GTIN, PLU, lot, or date-code mismatch is ignored and recalled or wrong product is picked, substituted, replenished, or sold
cold-chain, temperature, sanitation, allergen, contamination, or stop-sale evidence is treated as routine operational friction instead of a food-safety handoff
OMS, POS, payment capture, customer communication, and service-ticket records disagree and the lane closes or refunds the order anyway
out-of-policy refund, rain check, service credit, EBT/SNAP exception, or promotion exception is promised without authorized owner approval
repeated refund or high-value substitution claims with identity mismatches are handled as routine service recovery instead of loss-prevention routing
high-volume storm, holiday, or promotion work loses item-level, order-level, customer-level, and exception traceability
tenant-specific jurisdiction, franchise, tender, recall, food-safety, or refund controls are ignored in favor of generic grocery operations advice

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

required_workflow_questions_answered.what_booking_order_payment_or_guest_reco: OMS order records, pickup or delivery windows, staged tote and handoff records, POS receipts, payment authorization and capture records, tender exception records, CRM customer records, service-desk notes, refund-intake records, substitution preferences, loyalty records, and customer communications govern customer-facing progression. The lane cannot rely on screenshots, shopper notes, or verbal confirmation alone when OMS, POS, payment, inventory, or service records disagree.
required_workflow_questions_answered.what_refund_pricing_or_policy_constraint: FTC online order and refund guidance, NIST price verification and unit-pricing baselines, USDA FNS SNAP/EBT retailer rules, FDA and FSIS recall and safety records, PCI payment-data controls, GS1 item-identity standards, tenant refund windows, service recovery thresholds, promotion and coupon rules, franchise policies, jurisdictional weights-and-measures rules, food-safety SOPs, and loss-prevention controls limit the workflow. The lane may surface evidence and next steps but may not approve overrides outside delegated authority.
required_workflow_questions_answered.where_does_the_lane_stop_and_pricing_saf: The lane stops when the answer depends on price override, category strategy, refund or service-credit approval, rain-check exception, SNAP/EBT exception, payment dispute adjudication, fraud or loss-prevention decision, food-safety disposition, recalled-product release, legal interpretation, or executive policy override. Those conditions require adjacent specialist escalation or orchestrator return.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific weights-and-measures, unit-pricing, refund, bottle-deposit, food-code adoption, and SNAP/EBT implementation rules
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: State, local, tribal, franchise, or program requirements may be stricter than the public federal and model-code baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant-supplied jurisdiction, franchise, program, and policy overlays at runtime and escalate when they materially change the answer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant variation in substitution authority, service recovery, refund approval, cold-chain proof, recall execution, markdown controls, coupon abuse, and loss-prevention triggers
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Different retailers vary in when manager approval, item-level proof, customer contact, payment evidence, camera review, or loss-prevention review is mandatory.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat tenant SOPs, authority maps, and retrieval hooks as required runtime inputs before closing sensitive or customer-impacting cases.
